title: ArrangeSelections Object
description: Collection for all arrange selections to be passed to a CAMArrangeParameterValue object. This is a read-only container. It returns the arrange selections associated with the parent parameter value object, but does not write to it. To apply changes done to the collection and the selections it contains, CAMArrangeParameterValue.applyArrangeSelections() needs to be called.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createNewArrangeSelection
methods.description: Creates a new occurrence selection meant for arrange operations and adds it to the end of the collection.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified arrange selection object using an index into the collection.

methods.name: remove
methods.description: Function that removes the specified arrange selection object using an index in the collection.

methods.name: removeByObject
methods.description: Function that removes the specified arrange selection object from the collection.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of items in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

classType.title: ArrangeSelections.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createNewArrangeSelection.title: ArrangeSelections.createNewArrangeSelection Method
createNewArrangeSelection.description: Creates a new occurrence selection meant for arrange operations and adds it to the end of the collection.

item.title: ArrangeSelections.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified arrange selection object using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

remove.title: ArrangeSelections.remove Method
remove.description: Function that removes the specified arrange selection object using an index in the collection.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to remove. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

removeByObject.title: ArrangeSelections.removeByObject Method
removeByObject.description: Function that removes the specified arrange selection object from the collection.

parameters.name: selection
parameters.description: The item within the collection to remove. Throws an exception if the arrange selection is not part of the given selection.

count.title: ArrangeSelections.count Property
count.description: The number of items in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = arrangeSelections_var.count

isValid.title: ArrangeSelections.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = arrangeSelections_var.isValid

objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:

propertyValue = arrangeSelections_var.objectType
